description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses debian:9-slim as the default Docker image and runs a global before_script `apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3`. Define two stages: verify_chksum and sast. Add a job called `job 0` in the verify_chksum stage that runs only on tag pipelines, executes `apt-get install -y gnupg`, `gpg --import -v security-pubkey.asc`, and `scripts/verify-checksums.py` (after the global before_script). Add a job called `job 1` in the sast stage that overrides the image to docker:stable, uses the docker:stable-dind service, sets the variable DOCKER_DRIVER to overlay2, runs the same global before_script, then runs `export SP_VERSION=$(echo "$CI_SERVER_VERSION" | sed 's/^\([0-9]*\)\.\([0-9]*\).*/\1-\2-stable/')` followed by a `docker run` command that mounts the repository and the Docker socket, passes SAST_CONFIDENCE_LEVEL, and executes `/app/bin/run /code` from the security SAST image, and finally uploads `gl-sast-report.json` as a SAST artifact while allowing the job to fail. No caches or additional deployment targets are defined.
